text:
{
  "title": "Stress-Recovery Triad Profiles",
  "group_name": "stress_recovery_triad_profiles",
  "family": "behavioral_interaction",
  "summary": "Represent each student's rest, pressure, and movement pattern as a recovery-balance state that separates buffered high-demand lifestyles from depleted low-recovery lifestyles.",
  "depends_on": [],
  "strategy": "Map stress_level as low=0, medium=1, high=2; sleep_quality as poor=0, average=1, good=2; and physical_activity_level as sedentary=0, moderate=1, active=2. Bin sleep_duration into 0 for <6 hours, 2 for 6 to 8.5 hours, and 1 for >8.5 hours; bin step_count into 0 for <5000, 1 for 5000 to <10000, and 2 for >=10000; bin exercise_duration into 0 for <20 minutes, 1 for 20 to <60 minutes, and 2 for >=60 minutes. Clip numeric inputs to the train-observed valid ranges before binning; map missing numeric values, missing categories, and unknown categories to the neutral middle score 1. Define movement_score as the rounded mean of step, exercise, and reported activity scores; define rest_score as the rounded mean of sleep-duration and sleep-quality scores. Emit a 27-level integer triad code as 9 * stress_score + 3 * rest_score + movement_score, a recovery_margin as rest_score + movement_score - stress_score, stressed_buffered as 1 when stress_score is 2 and both rest_score and movement_score are at least 1, and exhausted_inactive as 1 when stress_score is at least 1 and both rest_score and movement_score are 0.",
  "expected_signal": "Student-health literature reports coupled associations among sleep quality, stress, and physical activity, including stress mediating the sleep-exercise relationship, so this group may expose non-linear class patterns where the same stress level implies different risk depending on recovery and movement context; references used include https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/public-health/articles/10.3389/fpubh.2025.1640974/full and https://www.mdpi.com/2227-9032/10/10/1930.",
  "risk": "The hand-built ordinal bins may be redundant with raw fields and the prior deviation-burden group, and neutral missing-value handling can blur informative missingness or impose a false middle state when nonresponse itself is class-specific."
}
